# Questions in Livestream 190

Live Stream #190: Spilt Tea All Over the Floor

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=496" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:16</a> | Did the worn temple steps at Hathor actually melt? | He said no. They are eroded stone, worn down by sand and foot traffic rather than heat or anything exotic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=576" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:36</a> | Is it wrong for Christians to amass wealth and enjoy luxury if some of that wealth is also used for God's kingdom? | He said wealth itself is not the problem; the issue is selfish, hedonistic, or unethical use of it rather than stewarding it under God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=1098" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:18</a> | Did `I AM` mean anything special to the Hebrews before God sent Moses? | He said no special divine meaning is evident before Moses, and treated it more as a verbal form than as an earlier sacred name among the Hebrews. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=1164" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:24</a> | Is Molech a deity or a type of child sacrifice, and was child sacrifice common in Ur? | He treated Molech as a type of sacrifice rather than a deity, and said there is evidence of human sacrifice in Ur but not of the same tophet pattern. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=1865" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:05</a> | What books or resources are good for Sumerian or Akkadian study? | He recommended good language texts, especially Ungnad for Akkadian, and noted that the Chicago Assyrian Dictionary is available online. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=2097" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:57</a> | Why can Akkadian not be written easily on parchment? | He explained that cuneiform is a stamped writing system made for clay and a stylus, not a flowing ink or parchment medium. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=2472" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:12</a> | In Exodus 16:3, did Israelite slaves actually eat meat and bread in Egypt? | He said they did have meat and bread, but as state-owned slaves they were expected to cultivate or produce much of it themselves. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=3604" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:04</a> | Does Egyptology have sub-specializations? | Yes. He listed field archaeologists, linguists, historians, ceramists, and other narrower tracks within Egyptology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=4324" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:04</a> | What antivirus program does the Faulk family use? | He said Windows Defender is usually enough, with Malwarebytes sometimes useful for cleanup, and criticized many antivirus suites as worse than helpful. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=4656" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:36</a> | Was Jonah's prayer said while he was dying or after he died? | He said it was while he was alive, pointing to Jonah 2's wording and past-tense literary features. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=4930" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:10</a> | Could 1 Peter 3:18-20 suggest the spirits in prison had a chance to trust Christ? | He treated the passage cautiously and said the text can suggest proclamation, but it does not clearly establish a simple second-chance doctrine. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=5362" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:22</a> | If Genesis 1:1 is a title separate from verse 2, why does Genesis 1:2 begin with a waw? | He said the Hebrew can still work as a simple waw and does not require verse 1 to be read as a dependent clause. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=7962" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:42</a> | How should the story of David and Bathsheba be viewed; was it abuse? | He said David clearly abused power in taking another man's wife and arranging Uriah's death, though the text does not explicitly describe Bathsheba as coerced. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=8121" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:21</a> | Why could David not control himself in the first place? | He said David could have, but he was a sinner with time on his hands and was looking where he should not have looked. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=8151" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:51</a> | How common was it for a king to walk on his roof and see a woman bathing at night? | He said roof bathing could be common in some ancient settings, and Bathsheba was on her own roof taking a private bath. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=8340" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:00</a> | If Amorites and Canaanites used terms like El and Elohim, did they share the same myths about Baal, Mot, Ashtart, and others? | He said it is possible but uncertain; shared divine names do not prove all the same myth cycles were held everywhere. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=8500" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:21:40</a> | Did the patriarchs have a moral code from God, or did they follow the local laws where they lived? | He said Abraham and Sarah seem to quote or follow Mesopotamian legal ideas, but the answer may differ among patriarchs. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=8900" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:20</a> | What is your take on Elijah's departure in 2 Kings 2:11? | He said the chariot imagery is less central than Elijah being taken up to heaven, and the passage should be read carefully rather than over-systematized. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=10833" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:00:33</a> | Is the boat-shaped formation on the mountain really Noah's Ark? | He said it is not; in his view it is one of several mud-flow formations and not evidence for an ark site. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=11073" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:04:33</a> | What would be the theological implications of alien life? | He said it would imply God created more life for relationship, much as God created humans and angels for relationship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=11858" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:17:38</a> | Why are there no Egyptian records of the Exodus? | He said records from the relevant Delta sites rarely survive because of high water tables and poor preservation, especially for papyrus. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=12198" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:18</a> | Could Egyptians join Israel after seeing the plagues? | He said yes, pointing to the mixed multitude in Exodus 12 as including people who were not descendants of Jacob. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=12377" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:26:17</a> | What caused current biblical illiteracy? | He said literacy is cyclical and shaped by consumed media, and the current cycle has produced weak biblical literacy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=13479" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:44:39</a> | Are the Exodus population numbers idealized or mistranslated? | He said the census numbers are controversial, and one major issue is how to interpret terms such as `eleph` in Numbers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=13752" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:49:12</a> | How would you respond to the claim that only the Romans wanted to kill Jesus and the Pharisees were blameless? | He said the Romans were not the only ones involved; the Jewish authorities treated Jesus' claims as blasphemy and brought him first to the Sanhedrin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QhyTbeOS4g4?t=14777" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:06:17</a> | Will the novel be available in ebook or audiobook format? | He said it would definitely be available as an ebook, paperback, and hardcover, while audiobook format was still uncertain. |
